header.eyebrow: Venue Information · Arches B C D And E, Montague Close SE1

city.title: CITY WORKER PROXIMITY
city.text: The Square Mile is within 10-minute walking distance. Corporate lunch, after-work and team-building bookings are a core revenue opportunity.

young.title: YOUNG PROFESSIONAL BASE
young.text: 48% of SE1 residents are aged 25–44 — the primary spending demographic for experience-led hospitality.

tourism.title: INTERNATIONAL TOURISM
tourism.text: Borough Market and The Shard draw millions of international visitors annually. No Dice Borough is a natural tourist magnet — an experience venue with no paid acquisition cost.

evening.title: EVENING ECONOMY
evening.text: London Bridge is a major night economy hub. Friday and Saturday footfall is exceptional with no enforced curfew on experience venues.

boroughMarket.name: Borough Market
boroughMarket.text: 15–20M annual visitors · One of Europe's most visited food destinations

southBank.name: South Bank
southBank.text: London-themed golf course ties to Tate, Shard & South Bank day outs

floorPlan.title: Floor Plan — Arches B C D And E, Montague Close SE1
floorPlan.storesNote: SPACE CURRENTLY USED FOR STORES, CELLAR & KITCHEN

hours.licensed: Licensed Activity Hours — 11:00 to 23:00 all days

development.licenceIntro: The current premises licence was granted for a golf-led activity venue. Four targeted variations would materially increase revenue potential. Zero enforcement history and an activity-led format are strong grounds for all four applications.

basement.title: Basement Space
basement.intro: The venue sits above 300m² of undeveloped basement space with full rights to sublet and carry out works. Expansion is structural upside already embedded in the lease.
basement.body: The basement represents significant embedded upside — it is not speculative. The rights exist, the space exists, and the structural costs are not borne by No Dice Borough Ltd.

yard.title: Yard Space & External Capacity
yard.intro: A significant external yard at the front of the venue is currently used by Boro Bistro under a landlord agreement. The opportunity to reclaim this space is live and represents a material uplift in capacity and bar revenue.
yard.currentBody: Situation is live — outcome subject to Boro Bistro's next infraction
yard.body: The yard is not speculative pipeline — it is an active situation. If Boro Bistro commit one further breach of their agreement, the opportunity to take back this space becomes available immediately.

licenceVariations.alcoholCurrent: Currently: Alcohol tied to golf ticket or arcade token purchase
licenceVariations.sportsCurrent: Currently: No live sports screenings permitted (Condition 353)
licenceVariations.recommendedBody: Do not apply for all four variations simultaneously — this signals a venue seeking to transform its character. Sequence the applications. A clean zero-incident trading record in SE1 is the strongest asset in every application.
licenceVariations.disclaimer: This analysis is for information only and does not constitute legal advice. Engage a licensed specialist solicitor before submitting any variation application to Southwark Council.
